w:r.w:t: Grains and Beans High-efficiency Grinding Solutions

This solution provides a comprehensive, high-efficiency, precision and dust-free milling solution for enterprises processing grains, cereals and pulses. Whether you are an individual user, a small-scale workshop or a large-scale processing enterprise, you can find a
or complete production line that suits your needs.
Low Cost, High Value for Money

has designed multiple configuration options tailored to the high-fibre, high-oil and high-hardness characteristics of coarse grains and pulses, meeting the milling needs of both individuals and businesses.
We have established a dust-free production line, achieving an outstanding dust suppression rate of up to 99.99% through our dust extraction system.
Consistently produces high-quality grain and legume flours with adjustable particle sizes ranging from 60 to 150 mesh, featuring uniform fineness to meet the diverse needs of our customers.
Electricity consumption per tonne of feedstock is reduced by up to 50%, with single-line production capacity reaching over 3 tonnes per hour, supporting 12 hours of continuous, stable operation.

Integrated intelligent control, with solutions covering the entire process from raw material cleaning and fine grinding to finished product collection, helping customers enhance product value, reduce operating costs and achieve clean production.
is not merely about selecting equipment; it is about securing long-term assurance for ‘peace of mind in production and satisfaction in the results’.
Introduction to Whole Grains and Coarse Cereals
Soya beans, mung beans, red beans, black beans, peas
Contain oil and protein; relatively tough
Bean flour, protein powder, soya milk ingredients
High in fibre; some with husks
Chinese yam, Poria cocos, Euryale seed, lotus seed
Varying degrees of hardness; some are high in starch
Oil-rich legumes: The grinding process generates heat and releases oil, leading to screen clogging
Whole grains with husks: High fibre toughness makes it difficult for standard equipment to grind them uniformly
High fineness requirements: Food-grade applications often require a mesh size of 100 or higher
Strict hygiene standards: The food and pharmaceutical industries require stainless steel contact parts
Infant and toddler food, health supplements
DoDoShark’s Technical Commitment: For grains, cereals and pulses, DoDoShark
flagship models can consistently produce fine powders of 100–150 mesh
, offering very high output and excellent value for money. Even at 150 mesh, our grinding process maintains industry-leading capacity and stability.
Open-air crushing or rudimentary bag-type dust collectors
Failure to meet environmental standards, 3–5% raw material wastage, and health risks to staff
Failure to meet particle size specifications
unable to achieve the specified fineness
Or, whilst the fineness is achieved, the output is too low to be cost-effective
Blockages caused by oil and fat materials
Standard equipment for processing pulses, sesame seeds, etc.
High-oil materials are prone to clogging the screens
Temperature rise during the grinding process
Electricity consumption per tonne of material reaches 30–40 kWh
Residual material is difficult to clean up
Failure to meet food/pharmaceutical certification standards
No one to contact if equipment malfunctions; spare parts are difficult to source
Maximum hourly output at 100 mesh: 100 kg
Suitable for processing: Small quantities of material
Maximum hourly output at 100 mesh: 3 tonnes
Suitable for processing: Large volumes of material
Maximum hourly output at 100 mesh: 200 kg
Suitable for processing: Small quantities of material
Maximum hourly output at 100 mesh: 1.5 tonnes
Suitable for processing: Large volumes of material
Maximum hourly output at 100 mesh: 60 kg
Maximum hourly output at 100 mesh: 1 tonne
Suitable for processing: Large volumes of material
We match the right machine models and solutions to different materials and requirements to achieve optimal efficiency
In-house production ensures high efficiency and low overall costs
Better value for money and superior performance
From design and manufacturing to installation and commissioning,
takes sole responsibility to ensure the system is perfectly matched and operates efficiently
Modular design allows for flexible expansion of production capacity or the addition of functional modules as your business grows
Material: Roasted soya beans, 0.6–0.8 mm mesh
304 stainless steel Model 37 + 7.5 kW motor + high-pressure fan + 4-bag dust collector
Results: Efficient processing of roasted soya beans, with an hourly output of 300 kg and excellent dust removal performance
Case Study 2: Busan Food Factory, South Korea
304 stainless steel Model 37 + 7.5 kW WEG variable-frequency motor + high-pressure fan + 5-bag dust collector
Results: Fine grinding, 150 kg per hour, energy-saving variable-frequency drive, trusted by international customers
